RULING: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
No opposition and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for order advancing the current trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d). No opposition filed. The February 11, 2019 trial date is advanced to December 17, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is December 27, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 13, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 30, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 14, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 28, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael J. Beler of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Sid Samora and Ronda Samora

RULING:
DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's general demurrer pursuant to C.C.P. 430.10(e) on the grounds that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71 by identifying the product(s) for which defendant is responsible. Defendant's general demurrer pursuant to C.C.P. 430.10(f) on the grounds that the complaint is uncertain is overruled as uncertainty is not a ground for a general demurrer. Defendant's general demurrer based on the statute of limitations is overruled. On its face, the complaint does not state facts demonstrating that the action is time-barred pursuant to C.C.P. 340.2. Defendant's request to stay discovery as related to defendant pending ruling on the demurrer is denied as moot as Mr. Samora's deposition was noticed for May 31, 2017. If Mr. Samora's deposition has not yet commenced, defendant's request to stay the deposition is granted only as related to defendant J-M Manufacturing. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Item 1 is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Items 2, 3 and 4 is granted to the extent the Court will take judicial notice of the existence of the documents, but not the truth of the matters asserted therein. Defendant's request for judicial notice of Item 5 is granted as it appears there is no opposition thereto. Defendant's demurrer complies with C.C.P. 430.60 and CRC 3.1112 and 3.1320. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
